2024-11-08.thesis: Amazon's nuclear SMR investment deal spurs sector surge
2024-11-08.exit_case: FERC rejected the Amazon-Talen nuclear data-center co-location deal, a concrete regulatory setback for the buildout narrative.
2024-11-08.assessment: This week brings the first clearly NEGATIVE, concrete news since entry: FERC rejected a proposed Amazon data-center nuclear co-location arrangement (Talen/Susquehanna), and nuclear stocks broadly sold off on the news. This is a distinct transaction from the original Amazon/X-energy SMR investment that is the actual entry catalyst, so it does not resolve or reverse the specific thesis — but it is real evidence that the 'smooth, unstoppable Big Tech nuclear buildout' narrative faces genuine regulatory friction, not just profit-taking. Combined with prior weeks' drift into mainstream 'load up' sell-side coverage and crowding, and now a concrete setback, conviction should step down meaningfully. The structural driver (AI data centers need power, hyperscalers pursuing nuclear) is not reversed — Constellation's 30% EPS beat and continued interest confirm fundamentals — but the easy, purely-bullish phase looks over and the arc is showing its first real friction. No new concrete catalyst (deal, vote, approval) is clearly scheduled ahead this week beyond continued speculation, reinforcing a fade rather than a hard exit. Maintain the basket but at reduced conviction; watch for either a reversal (more regulatory rejections / project cancellations) or a fresh positive catalyst (new SMR contract, approval) to resolve the ambiguity.
2024-11-08.exit_advice: Hold but trim conviction — this is a genuine negative datapoint (regulator pushback on grid co-location), not a reversal of the entry catalyst (Amazon's X-energy SMR investment) or of the structural Big-Tech-nuclear-power driver, but it confirms increasing friction as the theme matures and crowds. If further regulatory rejections or utility pullbacks follow, treat as arc-reversal and exit.
